title: REQ-FEED-028 'WHEN feed_initial_state is invoked SHALL create new note' implies auto-create on EVERY call, with destructive consequences for re-mounts and `confirm_note_deletion` rescans
description: The EARS clause reads: 'WHEN `feed_initial_state(vault_path)` が呼ばれる THE SYSTEM SHALL 新規 NoteId を採番し、空 body の Note エントリを `note_metadata` および `visibleNoteIds` の先頭に追加し、`editing.status = "editing"`、`editing.current_note_id = 新規NoteId` として `FeedDomainSnapshotDto` を返す.' This is unconditional: every invocation produces a new ghost ID. Problems: (1) The frontend may legitimately re-invoke `feed_initial_state` on AppShell remount, hot-reload during dev, or as a recovery action — each call leaks a new ghost ID and unconditionally hijacks `editing.current_note_id`, discarding whatever the user was just editing. (2) The spec explicitly says (line 931) 'ファイル I/O は scan のみ: 新規 `.md` ファイルは作成しない. `capture-auto-save` ハンドラが初回 save 時に作成する責務を持つ'. So consecutive `feed_initial_state` calls generate ghost IDs that are never persisted; each call discards the previous ghost. Cumulative ghost count if user types nothing: 1 per call. The spec does not state whether subsequent calls return the *same* ghost ID (idempotent) or always a fresh one. The TS reference `initializeCaptureSession` is documented as Step 4 of an AppStartup pipeline that runs 'once at startup' (workflows.md Workflow 1) — the Rust port loses that scoping. (3) REQ-FEED-022 Sprint 5 amendment (line 987) says `editing.status` is now `"editing"` 'Sprint 4 以前は `"idle"` であった可能性がある', without acknowledging that other call sites (Phase 1c spec does not enumerate them) may invoke `feed_initial_state` expecting an idle snapshot. The TS event taxonomy emits `NewNoteAutoCreated` once per session start — the Rust handler emits no such event, breaking any subscriber that depended on the TS Workflow 1 contract.
evidence:
behavioral-spec.md:915 — 'WHEN `feed_initial_state(vault_path)` が呼ばれる THE SYSTEM SHALL 新規 NoteId を採番し...'
behavioral-spec.md:931 — '新規 `.md` ファイルは作成しない. `capture-auto-save` ハンドラが初回 save 時に作成する責務を持つ'
behavioral-spec.md:987 — 'Sprint 4 以前は `"idle"` であった可能性がある'
promptnotes/src/lib/domain/app-startup/initialize-capture.ts:62 — emits `NewNoteAutoCreated` then `EditorFocusedOnNewNote` (no Rust equivalent in spec)
docs/domain/workflows.md:17 — Workflow 1 AppStartup is '同期、起動時 1 回' (sync, startup-only)
remediation: Add to REQ-FEED-028 either (a) an explicit precondition that `feed_initial_state` is invoked exactly once per AppShell mount and the TS frontend guarantees this (with an AC: `+page.svelte` invokes `feed_initial_state` only inside the Configured-state mount effect, never in re-renders), OR (b) an idempotency rule: if the vault already contains a note whose `body == ""` and `note_metadata` shows no other editing session, return that existing empty note instead of creating a fresh ghost (this implements 'session resume' semantics). Also explicitly state whether the Rust handler emits a `NewNoteAutoCreated`-equivalent event, or whether the TS frontend infers session start from `cause.kind == 'InitialLoad'` plus `editing.status == 'editing'`. Without one of these clarifications the spec is ambiguous about a destructive case (user's editing session being silently overwritten).
